verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent complex (LAP + mature TGFβ1) that is covalently tethered to cell-surface TM partners GARP (LRRC32) on Tregs/platelets and LRRC33 on myeloid cells via disulfide bonds. This wash-resistant covalent linkage to a TM scaffold places the latent TGFβ1 protein body on the outer leaflet of specific cell types. Clinical antibodies (e.g., anti-GARP/TGFβ1 complex, integrin αvβ8-blocking, isoform-selective latent TGFβ1 antibodies like SRK-181) target this surface-tethered form. Not yes because the dominant biological pool is soluble/latent and surface display requires the GARP/LRRC33 partner.
